meta.notes: All records included per downstream requirement. gate_status reflects mechanical gate checks only; editorial triage happens in Step 6.

models.name: GPT-5.5
models.gate_notes: All gates pass. Official Tier 1 sources (openai.com) + independent benchmark validation (BenchLM, Interesting Engineering). Concrete benchmarks: Terminal-Bench 2.0 82.7%, SWE-Bench Pro 58.6%. Released Apr 23 — technically outside collection window, but GPT-5.5 Instant (May 5, a separate record) is within window. Deprioritize pure GPT-5.5 base if editorial space is limited; prefer GPT-5.5 Instant record for within-window coverage.
models.editorial_flag: outside_window_borderline — Apr 23 release. Included as context for GPT-5.5 Instant; scorer may merge or deprioritize.

original_record.name: GPT-5.5
official_claims: Matches GPT-5.4 speed while delivering higher intelligence
82.7% on Terminal-Bench 2.0 (vs Claude Opus 4.7's 69.4%)
SWE-Bench Pro: 58.6%, OSWorld-Verified: 78.7%
60% fewer hallucinations vs GPT-5.4
Uses significantly fewer tokens to complete tasks (higher efficiency)
State-of-the-art on agentic coding, computer use, and scientific research
original_record.external_validation_summary: BenchLM ranks GPT-5.5 #3 overall (91/100) among 115 models with Arena Elo 1475. Leads on Terminal-Bench 2.0 by 13+ points over nearest competitor. Strong independent confirmation from multiple benchmark trackers.
original_record.real_change_notes: Significant agentic coding leap; beats Claude Opus 4.7 on terminal/computer-use tasks. More token-efficient than predecessors. Available in ChatGPT and Codex; API deployment announced.
original_record.selection_impact_notes: Likely to become default for agentic coding pipelines where terminal-use and multi-step tasks dominate. Relevant replacement consideration vs Claude Opus 4.7 for coding workflows.
original_record.price_speed_cost_notes: Pricing not yet disclosed for API; released to Plus/Pro/Business/Enterprise ChatGPT. Token efficiency improvement means lower effective cost per task.
original_record.app_api_workflow_notes: Available in ChatGPT and Codex at launch; API availability announced as coming soon. Tight integration with OpenAI Codex for agentic coding.
new_use_cases: Long-horizon agentic coding (20-hour senior dev tasks)
Computer use / desktop automation
Scientific research automation
Complex multi-step data analysis and document generation
original_record.business_model: Subscription (ChatGPT Plus/Pro/Business/Enterprise) + upcoming API pay-per-token
original_record.ecosystem_echo: Positioned as the top agentic coding model, directly competing with Claude Opus 4.7 for developer workflows. Significant industry attention.
original_record.durability_notes: Major frontier release; likely to hold top position for 2-3 months before next update cycle.
original_record.risk_caveat: API pricing not yet confirmed. System card notes red-teaming concerns for cybersecurity/biology misuse. Slightly outside Apr 29 – May 6 window (released Apr 23) but GPT-5.5 Instant (May 5) is within window.

good: Benchmark community confirms Terminal-Bench lead
Developers praising agentic task completion quality
Token efficiency praised (fewer tokens per task)
bad: API not yet available at launch (ChatGPT-only initially)
Pricing unknown for API

original_record.one_line_judgment: OpenAI's strongest agentic coding model yet, leading Terminal-Bench 2.0 at 82.7% and delivering higher intelligence at GPT-5.4 speeds.
original_record.relevance_to_us: High — directly relevant for AI-native creative tool workflows requiring agentic coding, computer use, and multi-step task execution. Strong candidate for skill/agent infrastructure backbone.

models.name: GPT-5.5 Instant
models.gate_notes: All gates pass. Official Tier 1 sources (openai.com, help.openai.com) + TechCrunch + VentureBeat validation. Concrete benchmark claims: 52.5% fewer hallucinations, AIME 2025 81.2, MMMU-Pro 76. May 5 release is squarely within window.

original_record.name: GPT-5.5 Instant
official_claims: 52.5% fewer hallucinated claims than GPT-5.3 Instant on high-stakes topics (medicine, law)
AIME 2025 math: 81.2 (vs 65.4 for GPT-5.3 Instant)
MMMU-Pro: 76 (vs 69.2 for GPT-5.3 Instant)
Enhanced image analysis and STEM question answering
Better personalization using past chats, memories, files, and Gmail
New Memory Sources feature shows what context shaped each response
original_record.external_validation_summary: TechCrunch and VentureBeat confirmed launch on May 5. Benchmark improvements independently noted. Memory Sources transparency feature widely covered.
original_record.real_change_notes: New default ChatGPT model as of May 5. Meaningful hallucination reduction. Memory Sources adds auditability. Within the April 29 – May 6 collection window.
original_record.selection_impact_notes: Replaces GPT-5.3 Instant as default for 200M+ ChatGPT users. Personalization improvements and memory sources are meaningful UX differentiator.
original_record.price_speed_cost_notes: Same pricing tier as predecessor. GPT-5.3 Instant available to paid users for 3 more months before retirement.
original_record.app_api_workflow_notes: Available via API as 'chat-latest'. Memory Sources UI in ChatGPT web/mobile (mobile rollout pending).
new_use_cases: High-stakes Q&A with reduced hallucination (legal, medical)
Personalized contextual assistance using multi-source memory
STEM and image analysis tasks
original_record.business_model: Free tier (default model) + Plus/Pro subscription + API pay-per-token
original_record.ecosystem_echo: Signals OpenAI's push toward persistent memory and personalization as core ChatGPT differentiator. Etsy app integration launched same day shows ecosystem momentum.
original_record.durability_notes: Default model replacement; impact is systemic across all ChatGPT users.
original_record.risk_caveat: Memory Sources described as 'not full auditability yet' — partial transparency only.

good: Hallucination reduction on sensitive topics well-received
Memory transparency (Sources) seen as useful trust feature
bad: Memory Sources doesn't show all factors that shaped an answer

original_record.one_line_judgment: ChatGPT's new default model with 52.5% fewer hallucinations and a new memory sources transparency layer — within collection window (May 5).
original_record.relevance_to_us: Medium-high — default model upgrade affects all ChatGPT integrations; memory + personalization patterns relevant for creative tool design.

models.name: Claude Opus 4.7
models.gate_notes: All gates pass. Official Tier 1 sources (anthropic.com) + Artificial Analysis + VentureBeat + llm-stats.com independent validation. Strong benchmark evidence: SWE-bench 87.6%, GPQA 94.2%, vision 3.3x resolution. Released Apr 16 — 13 days before window start. Key platform updates (Managed Agents Memory Apr 23, Rate Limits API Apr 24) also borderline. Included as editorially critical: current #1 SWE-bench GA model. Scorer should note the out-of-window context.
models.editorial_flag: outside_window — Apr 16 model release, Apr 23/24 platform updates. Editorially critical as the leading GA model; include with window caveat.

original_record.name: Claude Opus 4.7
official_claims: SWE-bench Verified: 87.6% (up from 80.8% for Opus 4.6)
GPQA Diamond: 94.2%
GDPval-AA: 1,753 Elo — leads GPT-5.4 and Gemini 3.1 Pro on knowledge work
High-resolution vision: images up to 2,576px (3.3x more than Opus 4.6), 98.5% visual-acuity
New xhigh effort level for deeper reasoning
Task budgets (beta) for token-bounded agentic loops
Self-verification: writes and runs tests before reporting results
35% fewer output tokens than Opus 4.6 while scoring 4 points higher on AI Index
original_record.external_validation_summary: Artificial Analysis confirms 35% fewer output tokens with higher quality. VentureBeat validates 'narrowly retaking lead for most powerful GA model'. Multiple independent benchmark trackers confirm SWE-bench 87.6%.
original_record.real_change_notes: Major capability jump on SWE-bench (+6.8pp), vision (3.3x resolution), and self-verification. Also adds Managed Agents Memory (Apr 23 — within window) and Rate Limits API (Apr 24 — within window).
original_record.selection_impact_notes: Best GA model for software engineering and knowledge work as of collection date. Vision improvements directly relevant for creative/design AI tools requiring image understanding.
original_record.price_speed_cost_notes: Unchanged at $5/$25 per million input/output tokens. 35% fewer output tokens = ~26% cost reduction in practice vs Opus 4.6.
original_record.app_api_workflow_notes: Available via Anthropic API, Amazon Bedrock, Google Cloud Vertex AI, Microsoft Foundry. New xhigh effort level and task budgets add workflow control. Managed Agents Memory (beta) launched Apr 23.
new_use_cases: High-resolution image analysis for creative tools (mockups, screenshots, design review)
Long agentic coding loops with self-verification
Multi-session work with file-system memory
Token-bounded agentic task completion with budgets
original_record.business_model: API pay-per-token ($5/$25 per M tokens) + Claude.ai subscription tiers
original_record.ecosystem_echo: Leading frontier model on SWE-bench and knowledge work. Adobe Firefly AI Assistant connector integrates Claude (Apr 28). Strong enterprise adoption via Bedrock/Vertex.
original_record.durability_notes: Top-of-class for 1-2 months; GPT-5.5 leads on terminal/computer-use tasks but Opus 4.7 leads on SWE-bench and knowledge work. Both will likely hold dual top positions.
original_record.risk_caveat: Released Apr 16 — 13 days before collection window start. Included as it remains the leading GA model and Claude platform updates (Managed Agents Memory Apr 23, Rate Limits API Apr 24) fall within window.

good: Self-verification feature widely praised for reducing coding errors
Vision resolution upgrade significant for design/image tasks
xhigh effort level gives finer control for expensive reasoning tasks
bad: API breaking changes from Opus 4.6 require migration
Still behind GPT-5.5 on terminal-bench and computer use

original_record.one_line_judgment: Anthropic's flagship retakes #1 SWE-bench at 87.6% with self-verification, 3.3x vision resolution, and 35% token efficiency gain.
original_record.relevance_to_us: Very high — top model for coding, vision tasks (creative tool images), and agentic loops. Self-verification and task budgets are directly applicable to skill/workflow systems.

models.name: Grok 4.3 + Voice APIs
models.gate_notes: All gates pass. Official Tier 1 sources (x.ai) + VentureBeat + TechSifted validation. Concrete claims: 50%+ price cut to $1.25/$2.50 per M tokens, 1M context, STT 5.0% entity error rate, TTS 5 voices 20+ languages. May 1 updated_at is within window; Custom Voices launched Apr 30 (within window). Note: full Grok 4.3 rollout mid-to-late May; currently SuperGrok Heavy beta ($300/mo). Voice API error benchmarks are self-reported — downstream scorer should apply hype penalty.

original_record.name: Grok 4.3 + Voice APIs
official_claims: Grok 4.3: built-in persistent reasoning, 1M token context, multimodal (text+image+video)
Aggressive pricing: $1.25/$2.50 per million input/output tokens (vs Grok 4.2's $2/$6)
Native document generation (PDFs, spreadsheets, PowerPoint)
Voice APIs: STT 5.0% entity error rate (vs ElevenLabs 12%, Deepgram 13.5%)
TTS: 5 voices, 20+ languages, expressive speech tags ([laugh], [sigh], [whisper])
Custom Voice cloning from short recordings (launched Apr 30)
Grok Voice Think Fast 1.0: enterprise voice agent for customer support (Apr 23)
original_record.external_validation_summary: VentureBeat and TechSifted confirm pricing and feature details. Voice API error rates cited from xAI's own benchmarks vs competitors — independent validation pending.
original_record.real_change_notes: 50%+ price cut vs Grok 4.2. Voice APIs and custom voice cloning are new product surface. Full Grok 4.3 rollout expected mid-to-late May 2026.
original_record.selection_impact_notes: Lowest priced 1M-context frontier model. Voice APIs (STT+TTS) open new interaction patterns for voice-enabled creative tools. Custom voice cloning is directly relevant for creative/media applications.
original_record.price_speed_cost_notes: $1.25/$2.50 per M tokens — significantly cheaper than GPT-5.5 (TBD) and Claude Opus 4.7 ($5/$25). STT: $0.10/hr batch, $0.20/hr streaming. TTS: $4.20 per 1M characters.
original_record.app_api_workflow_notes: Available via xAI API and OpenRouter. Beta for SuperGrok Heavy ($300/mo) subscribers; wider rollout May 2026. Voice APIs are standalone REST endpoints.
new_use_cases: Voice-enabled creative tool interfaces
Custom voice cloning for content creation
Low-cost 1M-context document analysis
Native presentation/slide generation
Enterprise voice agents for support/sales
original_record.business_model: API pay-per-token + SuperGrok subscription ($300/mo for heavy) + Grok on X platform
original_record.ecosystem_echo: Aggressive pricing pressures industry; integration with X platform and Tesla/Starlink infrastructure signals unique distribution. Custom Voices launched Apr 30 (within collection window).
original_record.durability_notes: Price leadership is durable advantage if maintained. Full rollout within collection window's tail.
original_record.risk_caveat: Voice API error benchmarks self-reported. Beta-only for highest capabilities (full rollout pending). Some features (video understanding) not yet widely available.

good: Aggressive pricing widely praised by developers
Voice quality (custom cloning) noted as impressive in early reviews
bad: Still in SuperGrok Heavy beta ($300/mo) for full Grok 4.3
Independent voice API benchmark validation lacking

original_record.one_line_judgment: xAI cuts Grok 4.3 price by 50%+ while launching a voice cloning suite — the cheapest 1M-context frontier model with the industry's first integrated STT+TTS+cloning stack.
original_record.relevance_to_us: High — price leadership and voice cloning APIs are directly relevant for creative tool builders. Custom voices enable new product forms in audio/media creation.

models.name: Mistral Medium 3.5
models.gate_notes: All gates pass. Official Tier 1 source (mistral.ai) + InfoQ + Winbuzzer validation. Concrete benchmarks: SWE-Bench 77.6%, τ³-Telecom 91.4%. Open weights on Hugging Face verifiable. May 2 release is within window. Modified MIT license noted for downstream commercial use review.

original_record.name: Mistral Medium 3.5
official_claims: 128B parameter dense model (not MoE), 256K context window
SWE-Bench Verified: 77.6%
τ³-Telecom agentic benchmark: 91.4%
Self-hostable on 4 GPUs
Configurable reasoning effort per request
Open weights under modified MIT license
Default model in Vibe CLI and Le Chat
original_record.external_validation_summary: InfoQ and multiple outlets confirm release and benchmark figures. Open weights on Hugging Face verifiable. SWE-Bench 77.6% is competitive but below Claude Opus 4.7 (87.6%) and GPT-5.5.
original_record.real_change_notes: First single-model combining instruction-following, reasoning, coding, and vision (vs separate specialized models). Open weights at frontier-competitive quality. Within collection window (May 2).
original_record.selection_impact_notes: Self-hostable frontier-competitive model changes economics for teams that want control over their inference. Vibe remote agents show practical agentic coding use case.
original_record.price_speed_cost_notes: $1.50/$7.50 per million input/output tokens via Mistral API. Can self-host on 4 GPUs — potentially $0 marginal cost for teams with GPU infrastructure.
original_record.app_api_workflow_notes: Available via Mistral API, Hugging Face, NVIDIA Build. Powers Vibe CLI remote agents and Le Chat Work Mode. Integrates with GitHub, Linear, Jira, Sentry, Slack, Teams.
new_use_cases: Self-hosted agentic coding pipelines
Remote cloud coding agents (Vibe)
Multi-tool work orchestration (Le Chat Work Mode)
Vision tasks with reasoning at self-hostable scale
original_record.business_model: API pay-per-token ($1.50/$7.50) + open weights (self-host) + Le Chat subscription
original_record.ecosystem_echo: Open weights strategy differentiates from GPT-5.5/Claude. Fills frontier-competitive open-weight gap left by DeepSeek V4's large hardware requirements.
original_record.durability_notes: Open-weight release creates lasting ecosystem effect; downstream fine-tuning and hosting will extend relevance.
original_record.risk_caveat: Modified MIT license (not fully permissive — check commercial terms). SWE-Bench 77.6% is good but not frontier-leading.

good: 4-GPU self-hosting requirement is accessible for small teams
Unified model (no need to route between specialist models) praised
bad: SWE-Bench below Claude Opus 4.7 and GPT-5.5
Modified MIT license terms need review for commercial use

original_record.one_line_judgment: Mistral's 128B open-weight model unifies reasoning, coding, and vision on 4 GPUs — the most accessible self-hostable frontier model, shipping with cloud coding agents on May 2.
original_record.relevance_to_us: High — self-hostable at 4 GPUs opens private deployment option for creative tool pipelines. Vibe remote agents pattern is directly applicable to skill/workflow infra.

models.name: DeepSeek V4
models.gate_notes: All gates pass. HuggingFace blog (Tier 2 — official NVIDIA/HuggingFace post) + NVIDIA developer blog validate technical specs. Open weights verifiable on HF. Concrete architectural claims: 1.6T params, 49B active/token, 27% of V3.2 FLOPs. Released Apr 24 — 5 days before window start. Borderline but architecture impact is significant. Scorer may apply brief-tier given time distance from window.
models.editorial_flag: outside_window — Apr 24 release, 5 days before window start. Preview status. Deprioritize if editorial space is constrained.

original_record.name: DeepSeek V4
official_claims: DeepSeek-V4-Pro: 1.6T total params, 49B active/token, 1M context, 384K output
DeepSeek-V4-Flash: 284B total params, 13B active/token, 1M context
Hybrid attention (CSA+HCA): 27% of V3.2 FLOPs for Pro, 10% for Flash
KV cache: ~2% the size of grouped query attention systems
MIT license open-source release
original_record.external_validation_summary: HuggingFace blog and NVIDIA confirm technical details. Open weights on Hugging Face verifiable. Efficiency claims (FLOP reduction, KV cache) are architectural — verifiable by inference practitioners.
original_record.real_change_notes: Massive efficiency breakthrough: 1M-context MoE at a fraction of predecessor FLOPs. Opens 1M-context inference to teams without H100 clusters. Released Apr 24 — borderline outside window.
original_record.selection_impact_notes: If FLOP/KV efficiency holds, DeepSeek V4 Pro could enable 1M-context inference on smaller GPU clusters. Flash variant is accessible for medium-scale teams.
original_record.price_speed_cost_notes: API via api.deepseek.com (pricing not confirmed in sources). Open weights allow self-hosting with dramatically reduced hardware vs V3/V3.2.
original_record.app_api_workflow_notes: Available on DeepSeek API, Hugging Face, chat.deepseek.com. V3/V3.2 API retire July 24, 2026.
new_use_cases: 1M-context document analysis on smaller GPU fleets
Long-context creative project management (entire codebases, scripts, storyboards)
Cost-efficient agentic inference at scale
original_record.business_model: API pay-per-token + open weights (MIT license, self-host)
original_record.ecosystem_echo: China-origin open-source model maintains competitive pressure on US labs. NVIDIA partnership validates inference efficiency claims.
original_record.durability_notes: MIT license + massive efficiency gain = durable ecosystem impact. Open-weight release will be fine-tuned and hosted by many downstream providers.
original_record.risk_caveat: Released Apr 24 — just outside collection window. Flagged as 'Preview' — pricing and routing may still shift. Very large model (865GB on-disk for Pro) limits accessibility.

good: Efficiency breakthrough widely celebrated in open-source community
1M context at 2% KV cache size of standard GQA is extraordinary
bad: 865GB on-disk for Pro requires massive storage
Preview status means production use is uncertain

original_record.one_line_judgment: DeepSeek V4 brings 1M-context MoE inference at 27% of predecessor FLOPs under MIT license — the open-weight efficiency benchmark for 2026.
original_record.relevance_to_us: Medium-high — 1M context + efficiency matters for long-form creative workflows. Open-weight and MIT license relevant for privacy-sensitive creative tool deployments.

models.name: MiniMax M2.7
models.gate_notes: All three gates pass: new model release with open weights, official Tier 1 source (minimax.io) + NVIDIA developer blog (Tier 2), concrete benchmarks cited with traceable URLs. However, released Apr 11 — 18 days before window start. Significantly outside time window. GDPval-AA benchmark is MiniMax's own proprietary measure. Self-evolution claims not yet independently verified. Scorer should heavily deprioritize or cap at brief given time distance.
models.editorial_flag: significantly_outside_window — Apr 11, 18 days before window start. Recommend brief or drop at scorer discretion.

original_record.name: MiniMax M2.7
official_claims: 230B total params, 10B active/token (sparse MoE), 204,800 context
SWE-Pro: 56.22% (near GPT-5.3-Codex performance)
VIBE-Pro: 55.6%, Terminal Bench 2: 57.0%
GDPval-AA: 1,495 Elo (highest among open-source for office work)
97% skill adherence while working with 40+ complex skills (>2,000 tokens each)
Self-evolution: model participated in its own post-training
Agent Teams for multi-agent collaboration
original_record.external_validation_summary: NVIDIA NIM availability and blog post validates technical specs. Open-source on Hugging Face verifiable. r/LocalLLaMA noted M2.7 as significant with 470 points/151 comments in April.
original_record.real_change_notes: Released Apr 11 — outside collection window. Notable for self-evolution claims and highest GDPval-AA (office work) among open-source models. Relevant for creative tool teams doing complex multi-tool orchestration.
original_record.selection_impact_notes: 97% skill adherence at 40+ skills is directly relevant for skill system design. Self-evolution post-training is a novel architectural claim worth watching.
original_record.price_speed_cost_notes: Available via NVIDIA NIM and Hugging Face — pricing per hosting provider. Relatively accessible MoE with 10B active params.
original_record.app_api_workflow_notes: NVIDIA NIM microservice, Hugging Face. Supports Agent Teams and dynamic tool search for agentic workflows.
new_use_cases: High-skill-count agentic orchestration (40+ skills)
Office document creation and editing at scale
Self-improving agent systems (post-training via self-evolution)
original_record.business_model: API via NVIDIA NIM + open weights on Hugging Face
original_record.ecosystem_echo: Strong r/LocalLLaMA community reception. NVIDIA partnership signals mainstream adoption. Self-evolution claim is a first in open-source.
original_record.durability_notes: Self-evolution and skill adherence claims set a benchmark others will aim to beat; watch for follow-up M2.8 or successor.
original_record.risk_caveat: Released Apr 11 — 18 days outside window. Self-evolution claims are novel and need longer-term validation. Office work ELO benchmark is Minimax's own measure.

good: r/LocalLLaMA: '470 points, 151 comments' reception — very positive
Office task performance praised for Excel/PowerPoint/Word fidelity
bad: Self-evolution claims not yet independently verified
GDPval-AA benchmark is proprietary to MiniMax

original_record.one_line_judgment: MiniMax M2.7 is the first open-source model to self-evolve during training and leads open-source office work benchmarks — strong signal for skill system architecture.
original_record.relevance_to_us: Medium — skill adherence research and self-evolution pattern directly informing our skill system design. Less urgent given Apr 11 release date.

models.name: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Nano Omni
models.gate_notes: All gates pass. HuggingFace blog (official NVIDIA post — Tier 2) is the primary source; Transformers v5.8.0 native support (GitHub, Tier 2) provides corroboration. Concrete claims: omni-modal (text/image/document/audio/video), long-context. Benchmark positions described but not independently verified — downstream scorer should apply hype penalty. Released Apr 28, 1 day before window start — borderline.
models.editorial_flag: borderline_window — Apr 28, 1 day before Apr 29 window start. Limited third-party validation. Acceptable at brief tier.

original_record.name: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Nano Omni
official_claims: Omni-modal: text, images, documents, audio, video understanding in one model
Top rankings on multiple multimodal leaderboards
Designed for document analysis, image reasoning, ASR, audio-video understanding, and computer use
Long-context multimodal intelligence for agents
Released April 28 (borderline in window)
original_record.external_validation_summary: HuggingFace blog (official NVIDIA post) confirms capabilities. Transformers library v5.8.0 added native support. Leaderboard positions not independently verified in sources.
original_record.real_change_notes: Omni-modal agent model from NVIDIA — positions as infrastructure-level model for document+audio+video agentic pipelines. Released Apr 28 — 1 day before window.
original_record.selection_impact_notes: Omni-modal document+audio+video in one model reduces pipeline complexity for creative tools handling mixed media inputs.
original_record.price_speed_cost_notes: Available via NVIDIA NIM and Hugging Face. Pricing per NIM endpoint (typically compute-based).
original_record.app_api_workflow_notes: NVIDIA NIM microservice + Hugging Face Transformers native support.
new_use_cases: Multi-modal creative asset analysis (documents, audio, video in one pass)
Agent computer use with document and audio understanding
Omni-modal RAG pipelines for creative archives
original_record.business_model: NVIDIA NIM enterprise compute + open weights
original_record.ecosystem_echo: NVIDIA's push into foundation model space signals GPU vendor vertical integration.
original_record.durability_notes: NVIDIA's distribution and NIM infrastructure ensures wide deployment.
original_record.risk_caveat: Benchmark positions described but not fully cited in available sources. One day outside collection window.
original_record.one_line_judgment: NVIDIA's omni-modal agent model handles documents, audio, and video in one inference call — reduces pipeline complexity for creative multi-media AI applications.
original_record.relevance_to_us: Medium — omni-modal pipeline pattern relevant for creative tools ingesting mixed media. Less frontier-critical than GPT-5.5/Claude Opus 4.7.

models.name: Gemini API Multimodal File Search (Gemini Embedding 2)
models.gate_notes: All gates pass. Official Tier 1 source (blog.google) with concrete claims: text+image RAG in one API, custom metadata filtering, page-level citations. Released May 5 — within window. Note: single official source only, no independent third-party validation. Not a standalone model release but an API capability addition backed by Gemini Embedding 2 model. Scorer should note single-source limitation and apply hype penalty accordingly.
models.editorial_flag: single_source_caution — only official Google blog; no independent validation yet. API capability, not a full model release.

original_record.name: Gemini API Multimodal File Search (Gemini Embedding 2)
official_claims: File Search now multimodal: text+image RAG in one API
Powered by Gemini Embedding 2 model
Custom metadata filtering for unstructured data organization
Page-level citations for improved transparency and grounding
Released May 5, 2026 — within window
original_record.external_validation_summary: Official Google blog post confirms. Gemini Embedding 2 powering the feature is the key technical signal — embedding model upgrade enables multimodal retrieval.
original_record.real_change_notes: Within collection window (May 5). Enables multimodal RAG without separate embedding pipelines. Reduces developer complexity for multi-format document search.
original_record.selection_impact_notes: Relevant for creative tool builders using Gemini API for document+image RAG (design assets, visual references, mixed-media archives).
original_record.price_speed_cost_notes: Gemini API pricing applies. Embedding 2 pricing not separately listed.
original_record.app_api_workflow_notes: REST API via Gemini API. Custom metadata filtering enables structured retrieval from unstructured visual+text content.
new_use_cases: Multimodal creative asset retrieval (search images and text together)
Visual design archive search
Mixed-format document RAG for product catalogs, design systems
original_record.business_model: Gemini API pay-per-use via Google Cloud
original_record.ecosystem_echo: Signals Google's push to make Gemini the default embedding+RAG stack for enterprise developers.
original_record.durability_notes: API capability additions are sticky — once developers build on multimodal File Search, switching cost is high.
original_record.risk_caveat: Limited third-party validation available; Google-sourced only. Not a model release per se — API/embedding model upgrade.
original_record.one_line_judgment: Google's Gemini API adds multimodal file search (text+image RAG) on May 5, powered by Gemini Embedding 2 — directly useful for creative asset retrieval pipelines.
original_record.relevance_to_us: Medium-high — multimodal RAG API directly useful for creative tools that need to search visual + text content together.

products.name: ChatGPT Memory Sources + Excel/Sheets Integration
products.gate_notes: Both gates pass. Available to all ChatGPT users as of May 5 (new default model + Memory Sources UI). Multiple independent sources: official OpenAI changelog + TechCrunch + VentureBeat. Fully within window.

original_record.name: ChatGPT Memory Sources + Excel/Sheets Integration
original_record.launch_signal: May 5, 2026 ChatGPT release — new default model (GPT-5.5 Instant) + Memory Sources transparency + Excel/Sheets integration GA
original_record.core_positioning: ChatGPT becomes a persistent, personalized AI assistant that shows its reasoning sources and works natively inside productivity apps
original_record.problem_it_solves: Users repeat context every session; opaque AI memory; AI assistance limited to the chat window instead of where work happens
original_record.user_visibility: High — affects all ChatGPT users as new default model and visible Memory Sources UI in every personalized response
original_record.access_barrier_change: Excel/Google Sheets integration GA globally (previously limited). Memory Sources available across all consumer plans.
original_record.workflow_change: Users can now see which memories/files/Gmail shaped their ChatGPT response and edit outdated context. Spreadsheet work happens inside the tool, not copy-paste between apps.
original_record.distribution_change: Excel and Google Sheets sidebar integration puts ChatGPT inside the world's most-used productivity apps
original_record.new_product_form: Memory-auditable AI assistant + embedded spreadsheet AI agent
original_record.interaction_pattern: Tap 'sources' button on personalized responses to audit and edit what the model remembered. Natural language in sidebar to build/fix spreadsheets.
user_scenarios: Content creator asks ChatGPT to write in their style — checks which past conversations it used, removes outdated preferences
Marketer builds a budget tracker directly in Google Sheets using natural language ChatGPT sidebar
Designer uses ChatGPT to understand/fix a complex Excel formula without leaving the spreadsheet
original_record.business_model: Free (basic) + Plus/Pro/Business/Enterprise subscriptions; Excel/Sheets usage limits per plan tier
original_record.risk_caveat: Memory Sources described as 'not full auditability yet' — users may over-trust AI memory transparency. Gmail integration raises privacy concerns.

good: Memory Sources transparency seen as trust-building feature
Spreadsheet integration GA praised for productivity workflow unification
bad: Memory transparency is partial — not all factors shown
Gmail integration opt-in but privacy-sensitive

original_record.market_signal_strength: Very high — affects 200M+ ChatGPT users on May 5 (within collection window)
original_record.relevance_to_us: High — Memory Sources pattern is directly applicable to skill/workflow product design. Transparency of AI reasoning/context is a key UX challenge we face.
original_record.one_line_judgment: ChatGPT adds auditable memory sourcing and global spreadsheet integration on May 5 — a meaningful step toward persistent, transparent AI assistance at mass scale.

products.name: Adobe Firefly AI Assistant
products.gate_notes: Both gates pass. Public beta rolling out to Creative Cloud subscribers — user-accessible today. Multiple independent sources: Adobe blog + TechCrunch + 9to5Mac. Public beta launched Apr 27 (2 days before window start); Adobe for Creativity connector launched Apr 28. Editorially critical for creative AI coverage — included despite borderline timing. Note: beta status means production reliability unconfirmed.
products.editorial_flag: borderline_window — public beta Apr 27, connector Apr 28. Both 1-2 days before Apr 29 window start. Editorially critical; include with timing caveat.

original_record.name: Adobe Firefly AI Assistant
original_record.launch_signal: Public beta April 27, 2026; announced April 15. Adobe for Creativity connector in Claude launched April 28.
original_record.core_positioning: AI creative agent that orchestrates multi-step workflows across 60+ Creative Cloud tools from a conversational interface
original_record.problem_it_solves: Multi-step creative workflows require switching between apps (Photoshop, Premiere, Lightroom, Illustrator) manually; AI was confined to single-tool assistants
original_record.user_visibility: High — public beta rolling out to Creative Cloud subscribers. Adobe for Creativity connector brings tools into Claude interface.
original_record.access_barrier_change: Public beta (previously private preview). Creative Cloud subscription required. Adobe for Creativity connector available in Claude (no separate Adobe login needed).
original_record.workflow_change: Describe outcome in natural language → assistant executes multi-app workflow → user reviews and refines. Pre-built skills for batch editing, mood boards, portrait retouching, social variations, product mockups.
original_record.distribution_change: Adobe tools now accessible inside Claude via connector (Apr 28) — extends reach beyond Adobe subscribers to Claude users
original_record.new_product_form: Creative agent that spans multiple professional creative apps (not a single-app assistant)
original_record.interaction_pattern: Conversational interface with context-aware controls; user stays in control throughout; outputs remain editable in native Adobe formats
user_scenarios: Social media manager describes 'generate 5 Instagram variations of this product photo' — agent uses Photoshop + Firefly across batch
Video editor asks for 'add music and color grade this footage' — agent uses Premiere + Lumetri + Firefly Audio
Claude user retouches photo using Adobe for Creativity connector without switching to Photoshop
original_record.business_model: Creative Cloud subscription (All Apps plan) — no separate AI fee announced for beta
original_record.risk_caveat: Public beta may have quality/reliability gaps. 60+ tools = complex orchestration surface area prone to errors. Borderline window (Apr 27/28 vs Apr 29 window start).

good: TechCrunch and 9to5Mac coverage emphasize multi-app orchestration as significant advance
Anthropic partnership (Claude connector) signals enterprise AI legitimacy
bad: Beta status means production reliability unknown
Creative Cloud subscription cost ($60/mo All Apps) limits accessibility

original_record.market_signal_strength: Very high — Adobe has 33M Creative Cloud subscribers; multi-app creative agent is category-defining
original_record.relevance_to_us: Very high — directly competitive and informative for our AI-native creative tool positioning. Multi-step creative workflow agent with skill-based pre-built actions is the exact product paradigm we are building toward.
original_record.one_line_judgment: Adobe's Firefly AI Assistant orchestrates multi-step workflows across 60+ Creative Cloud apps from natural language — the first serious enterprise creative agent at scale.

products.name: Mistral Vibe Remote Agents + Le Chat Work Mode
products.gate_notes: Both gates pass. Vibe CLI and Le Chat Pro are accessible to users as of May 2. Multiple sources: Mistral official blog + InfoQ + Mistral docs. May 2 launch is within window.

original_record.name: Mistral Vibe Remote Agents + Le Chat Work Mode
original_record.launch_signal: May 2, 2026 — Mistral Medium 3.5 public preview + remote cloud agents in Vibe + Le Chat Work Mode all launched together
original_record.core_positioning: Cloud-based async coding agents that run while users walk away + enterprise work orchestration in Le Chat
original_record.problem_it_solves: Local coding agents require keeping laptop open and block the machine; enterprise workers need multi-tool orchestration (GitHub, Linear, Slack, Teams) in one interface
original_record.user_visibility: Medium — Vibe CLI users + Le Chat Pro subscribers; open weights allow self-deployment
original_record.access_barrier_change: Remote agents open cloud execution beyond local CLI. Le Chat Work Mode available to Pro subscribers. Open-weight model enables self-hosted deployments.
original_record.workflow_change: Teleport local coding session to cloud → close laptop → get notification when agent finished with draft PR. Le Chat Work Mode: describe complex multi-step task → parallel tool calls → consolidated output.
original_record.distribution_change: Integration with GitHub, Linear, Jira, Sentry, Slack, Teams extends Vibe agents into enterprise workflows
original_record.new_product_form: Async cloud coding agent with state preservation + enterprise work orchestration chat
original_record.interaction_pattern: CLI command to teleport session to cloud; notifications on completion; Le Chat: natural language request with tool integrations auto-routed
user_scenarios: Developer pushes complex refactor task to Vibe cloud agent before a meeting — returns to find draft PR ready
PM asks Le Chat Work Mode to research competitive landscape, summarize in Notion, and create Linear tickets — agent executes all in parallel
Small team self-hosts Mistral Medium 3.5 on 4 GPUs for private coding agent
original_record.business_model: Le Chat Pro subscription + Vibe CLI enterprise plan + Mistral API ($1.50/$7.50 per M tokens)
original_record.risk_caveat: Cloud agent sandbox isolation needs security validation for enterprise use. Work Mode quality dependent on Mistral Medium 3.5 reasoning limits.

good: Async cloud execution pattern widely seen as the right UX for long-running coding tasks
Open-weight backing means teams can verify/audit agent behavior
bad: Vibe still less known than Cursor/Windsurf in developer community
Enterprise integrations (Jira, Linear) need proven reliability

original_record.market_signal_strength: Medium-high — async cloud coding agent is a meaningful product pattern, but Mistral's consumer reach is smaller than OpenAI/Anthropic
original_record.relevance_to_us: Very high — async cloud coding agent pattern directly applicable to our skill/workflow system design. Self-hosted option + enterprise integrations are key workflow infrastructure signals.
original_record.one_line_judgment: Mistral launches async cloud coding agents on May 2 — developers teleport sessions to the cloud, walk away, and get draft PRs back powered by an open-weight model.

products.name: Cursor 3.x — Agents Window, Fleet Management, Team Marketplace
products.gate_notes: Both gates pass. Multiple in-window releases: Team Marketplace (May 1), Enterprise model controls (May 4) — directly accessible to Cursor users. Sources: official changelog + blog + Nivaa Labs analysis. High relevance for agent/skill/workflow infra editorial focus.

original_record.name: Cursor 3.x — Agents Window, Fleet Management, Team Marketplace
original_record.launch_signal: Multiple releases within window: May 1 (Team Marketplace), May 4 (Enterprise model controls + usage analytics). Cursor 3 core (Apr 2) provides context.
original_record.core_positioning: AI IDE evolving into agent fleet management — developers run and manage multiple parallel AI agents instead of writing code directly
original_record.problem_it_solves: Developers limited to one agent at a time, requiring active supervision; admins lack control over model access and spend
original_record.user_visibility: High — Cursor has large developer mindshare. Team Marketplace and Enterprise controls affect team leads and admins.
original_record.access_barrier_change: Team Marketplace (May 1): admins can now create team plugin bundles without connecting a repo first. Enterprise model access controls (May 4) give granular allow/blocklists.
original_record.workflow_change: Agents Window: run multiple parallel agents (local, worktree, cloud, SSH) in tiled layout. Seamless handoff between local and cloud. Launch agents from mobile/web/Slack/GitHub.
original_record.distribution_change: Agent launch from Slack, GitHub, Linear (mobile + external) extends Cursor beyond the IDE window
original_record.new_product_form: Agent fleet manager with marketplace for bundled skills/rules/MCPs + enterprise governance layer
original_record.interaction_pattern: Tiled agent sessions in IDE; Agents Window sidebar for monitoring parallel runs; admins set model policies; Team Marketplace distributes curated skill bundles
user_scenarios: Developer runs 3 parallel agents: one fixing tests, one writing docs, one implementing a feature — monitors all in tiled view
Engineering manager sets team-wide model blocklist and soft spend limits with 50%/80%/100% alerts
Team lead creates a Marketplace package bundling company-specific MCP servers, rules, and skills — marks as Required for all team members
original_record.business_model: Pro ($20/mo) + Business/Enterprise tiers with admin controls; Cloud Agents billed per compute
original_record.risk_caveat: Agent fleet quality depends on underlying model quality; parallel execution adds complexity and cost. Enterprise controls are new (May 4) — reliability track record limited.

good: Agent fleet paradigm widely seen as the right direction for developer productivity
Tiled layout and Team Marketplace praised for team coordination
bad: Increased complexity of managing multiple agents simultaneously
Cloud agent costs can accumulate quickly without careful spend controls

original_record.market_signal_strength: High — Cursor is market-leading AI IDE with strong developer mindshare. Fleet management + marketplace signal a platform play.
original_record.relevance_to_us: Very high — Cursor's Team Marketplace (bundled skills/MCP/rules) is directly analogous to our skill system product vision. Fleet management pattern is directly applicable to our agent/workflow infrastructure.
original_record.one_line_judgment: Cursor ships a Team Marketplace for bundled skills and enterprise model governance on May 1-4 — signaling the AI IDE is becoming a platform for skill/agent distribution.

products.name: Perplexity Health + Premium Health Sources
products.gate_notes: Both gates pass. Available to Pro/Max subscribers since May 1-5; multiple independent sources confirm. However, health vertical is outside editorial focus per editorial focus filter — max tier = brief. The Perplexity Personal Computer (background OS-level AI agent) pattern within this record IS relevant for creative/workflow tool design and should be surfaced by scorer.
products.editorial_flag: editorial_deprioritize — health vertical outside core scope. Max tier: brief. Extract Personal Computer/background-agent pattern for brief mention.

original_record.name: Perplexity Health + Premium Health Sources
original_record.launch_signal: May 1 (Health platform GA), May 5 (Premium Health Sources with NEJM/BMJ integration). Personal Computer for Mac (Apr 16) adds context.
original_record.core_positioning: Perplexity Health unifies personal health data from wearables/EHRs/labs with clinician-grade medical knowledge for AI-assisted health management
original_record.problem_it_solves: Health data is fragmented across devices and apps; AI health answers lack peer-reviewed clinical backing
original_record.user_visibility: Medium — Pro/Max subscribers in US (May rollout). Premium Health Sources adds NEJM, BMJ integration.
original_record.access_barrier_change: Pro/Max subscription required. iOS and web. Oura and Function Health integrations coming soon.
original_record.workflow_change: Apple Health, Fitbit, Ultrahuman, Withings, Clue data unified into AI health assistant. Premium sources add clinical-grade answers.
original_record.distribution_change: Personal Computer for Mac (Apr 16) enables 24/7 background AI across local apps. Computer for Enterprise ($325/seat/mo) targets enterprise.
original_record.new_product_form: Unified personal health + clinician-grade knowledge AI (not just a search engine)
original_record.interaction_pattern: Conversational health queries backed by personal wearable data + peer-reviewed medical journals
user_scenarios: User asks about sleep trend from Oura ring data — health AI answers with wearable data + clinical context
Designer managing chronic condition gets AI-assisted medication question answered with NEJM-sourced context
original_record.business_model: Pro/Max subscription. Computer for Enterprise: $325/seat/month.
original_record.risk_caveat: Health AI carries liability risk; medical advice disclaimers essential. US-only rollout limits reach. Excluded from our main focus (health vertical) unless it informs creative/productivity patterns.

good: Premium health sources (NEJM, BMJ) seen as meaningful trust signal
Wearable data integration praised for personalized health context
bad: US-only limits global relevance
Health AI liability concerns persist

original_record.market_signal_strength: Medium — health vertical is strong signal but outside our core creative/productivity focus
original_record.relevance_to_us: Low-medium — health vertical is excluded per scope. However, Perplexity Computer (Personal + Enterprise) pattern of 24/7 background OS-level AI agent is highly relevant for creative workflow tools.
original_record.one_line_judgment: Perplexity Health launches with NEJM/BMJ integration on May 5 — clinician-grade AI health assistant, but the more relevant signal is Personal Computer's 24/7 background agent model for workflow tools.

products.name: Midjourney V8.1 Alpha — Image + Video Expansion
products.gate_notes: Both gates pass. V8 accessible at alpha.midjourney.com; Video V1 in web beta — user-perceivable today. Multiple sources: official Midjourney updates + Art and Algorithms + Startup Fortune analysis. However, V8 launched March 2026 and V8.1 Alpha released Apr 14 — 15 days before window start. Significantly outside window. Highly relevant for creative tool editorial focus. Scorer should deprioritize in ranking but note the platform-level significance for creative/video AI.
products.editorial_flag: significantly_outside_window — V8.1 Alpha Apr 14, 15 days before window start. Highly relevant creative AI signal; include as brief at minimum with timing caveat.

original_record.name: Midjourney V8.1 Alpha — Image + Video Expansion
original_record.launch_signal: V8 Alpha: March 2026. V8.1 Alpha: April 14, 2026. Video V1 in web beta. Video V2 in development.
original_record.core_positioning: Midjourney transitions from image-only to an image+video creative platform with 5x faster generation and 2K native resolution
original_record.problem_it_solves: Creators need video generation alongside image generation without switching tools; current AI video tools are expensive or slow
original_record.user_visibility: Medium — V8 at alpha.midjourney.com (separate from production); V7 still default on main site
original_record.access_barrier_change: Alpha access at alpha.midjourney.com. Video V1 in web beta. Pricing: ~25x cheaper than commercial AI video tools (1 credit per second of video = roughly the same as an image).
original_record.workflow_change: Animate button in 2026 Web Editor converts images to 21-second video clips. V8 brings faster iteration (5x) for image exploration.
original_record.distribution_change: Web Editor with integrated animate button centralizes image+video creation in one interface. Video V2 planned to extend clip lengths further.
original_record.new_product_form: Integrated image + video creative tool (vs separate tools like Runway/Kling)
original_record.interaction_pattern: Image generation with V8 (5x faster, 2K native, better text) → Animate button → 21s video. Moodboard/style reference workflow preserved.
user_scenarios: Graphic designer creates product mockup in V8, animates it into a 21-second social video at 25x lower cost than Runway
Creative director uses V8's better text rendering for typographic design generation
Brand team generates image + animated version in one tool without Runway subscription
original_record.business_model: Subscription-based credit system. Standard plans: $10-120/mo. Video ~1 credit/second (same as images).
original_record.risk_caveat: V8 still alpha (not production). V8.1 alpha released Apr 14 — outside the collection window start (Apr 29). Video V2 not yet released. V7 remains production default.

good: 5x speed improvement and 2K native resolution widely praised
Video pricing (~25x cheaper than competitors) seen as major differentiator
bad: Alpha status means V8 quality inconsistent vs V7 production
Video V1 limited to 21 seconds; V2 (longer clips) not yet shipped

original_record.market_signal_strength: High — Midjourney has largest AI image creation user base. Image+video integration at low cost is a significant market shift.
original_record.relevance_to_us: High — image+video creative platform integration is directly relevant for our creative tool stack. Pricing model (per-credit video = same as image) is an important reference point.
original_record.one_line_judgment: Midjourney V8.1 Alpha ships 5x faster 2K image generation and video creation at 25x lower cost than Runway — consolidating image+video creative production in one tool.

products.name: xAI Grok Voice Think Fast 1.0 + Custom Voice Cloning
products.gate_notes: Both gates pass. Custom Voices (Apr 30) and API access are publicly available. Multiple sources: x.ai official + VentureBeat + aitooltier. Custom Voices launched Apr 30 — within collection window. Voice cloning from short recordings is a concrete, user-accessible product form. Scorer should note that error rate benchmarks are self-reported.

original_record.name: xAI Grok Voice Think Fast 1.0 + Custom Voice Cloning
original_record.launch_signal: April 23: Grok Voice Think Fast 1.0. April 30: Custom Voices + Voice Library (within collection window). Voice APIs (STT+TTS) launched April 17.
original_record.core_positioning: Real-time enterprise voice AI stack with custom voice cloning — from short recordings to production-ready voices across 20+ languages
original_record.problem_it_solves: Enterprise voice agents require separate vendors for STT, TTS, and voice cloning; creative tools need affordable custom voice generation
original_record.user_visibility: Medium — developer API access; Grok Voice Think Fast for enterprise ($300/mo SuperGrok Heavy or API)
original_record.access_barrier_change: Custom Voices (Apr 30) allows anyone with short audio recording to clone a voice. API access for STT/TTS. Think Fast 1.0 for enterprise.
original_record.workflow_change: Developers integrate xAI voice stack as single vendor for STT+TTS+cloning. Custom voice cloning from short recordings eliminates studio recording requirements.
original_record.distribution_change: Same infrastructure powers Grok, Tesla, Starlink — commercial reliability signal. API available via xAI and OpenRouter.
original_record.new_product_form: Integrated voice AI platform (STT + TTS + cloning) at aggressive price points
original_record.interaction_pattern: Short audio clip → Custom Voice → TTS with expressive tags ([laugh], [sigh], [whisper]); STT with word-level timestamps and speaker diarization
user_scenarios: Podcast creator clones their voice from 30-second recording — generates voiced episodes from text scripts
Game studio creates 5 character voices with custom cloning — no voice actor sessions needed
Creative tool adds voice interface using xAI STT/TTS as single vendor at $0.10/hr STT + $4.20/1M chars TTS
original_record.business_model: API: STT $0.10-0.20/hr, TTS $4.20/1M characters. SuperGrok Heavy $300/mo includes Grok Voice Think Fast.
original_record.risk_caveat: Voice cloning raises ethical/consent concerns. Error rates from xAI benchmarks (self-reported). Think Fast 1.0 enterprise pricing is high.

good: Custom voice cloning from short recordings seen as major creative tool enabler
Aggressive STT/TTS pricing vs ElevenLabs praised
bad: Voice cloning ethics and consent framework not clearly stated
Self-reported error rate benchmarks need independent validation

original_record.market_signal_strength: Medium-high — voice cloning from short recordings at low cost is a meaningful creative tool enabler
original_record.relevance_to_us: High — custom voice cloning + STT/TTS API stack directly relevant for audio/voice features in creative tools. Within-window launch (Apr 30 Custom Voices).
original_record.one_line_judgment: xAI's Custom Voice Cloning (Apr 30) enables voice generation from short recordings at $4.20/1M chars — a significant cost reduction for creative voice production.

products.name: Etsy ChatGPT App Integration
products.gate_notes: user_can_perceive_today passes — Beta available to ChatGPT users. FAILS evidence_beyond_single_source: source_secondary is empty; only TechCrunch article with no corroborating official Etsy/OpenAI page or other media. Single-source mention with no independent corroboration → watchlist. May 5 is within window. Relevant as platform signal for ChatGPT-as-commerce-platform trend.
products.editorial_flag: watchlist — single source. If corroborating sources emerge, promote to pass.

original_record.name: Etsy ChatGPT App Integration
original_record.launch_signal: May 5, 2026 — Etsy launches native app inside ChatGPT, enabling 100M+ listing search via natural language
original_record.core_positioning: Native ChatGPT app that converts natural language creative/product discovery into Etsy marketplace search
original_record.problem_it_solves: Traditional keyword search doesn't match how creatives describe what they want; discovery of handmade/creative items requires browsing
original_record.user_visibility: High — available to all ChatGPT users (beta). Etsy has 100M+ listings.
original_record.access_barrier_change: Beta — available to ChatGPT users without separate Etsy account (browse-only). Full purchasing requires Etsy account.
original_record.workflow_change: Natural language: 'find a ceramic mug with an abstract design for under $30' → ChatGPT uses Etsy app to return matching products
original_record.distribution_change: Etsy gains ChatGPT distribution channel (200M+ users). Signal of ChatGPT becoming a platform for retail/commerce apps.
original_record.new_product_form: ChatGPT native commerce app (not just link-out, but integrated product search inside the chat)
original_record.interaction_pattern: Describe product in natural language inside ChatGPT → Etsy app searches 100M+ listings → results appear in chat
user_scenarios: Creative director searches 'handmade stamps for my zine project' in ChatGPT — Etsy results appear without leaving the chat
Designer briefs ChatGPT on a project mood → asks it to find relevant handmade materials from Etsy
original_record.business_model: Etsy commission model on transactions driven via ChatGPT channel. ChatGPT platform revenue via OpenAI.
original_record.risk_caveat: Beta status. Commerce in chat is early pattern — conversion rate unknown. Excludes most creative professional tools (only consumer marketplace).

good: Natural language product discovery in ChatGPT seen as intuitive for creative users
bad: Beta — limited features, no direct purchase
Etsy's AI push is broader trend, not isolated to ChatGPT

original_record.market_signal_strength: Medium — strong signal for ChatGPT-as-platform trend; limited direct creative tool relevance
original_record.relevance_to_us: Low-medium — signals ChatGPT becoming a platform for third-party apps (relevant for our potential distribution). Etsy's creative marketplace is tangentially relevant to our creative tool audience.
original_record.one_line_judgment: Etsy launches a native ChatGPT app on May 5 — natural language discovery across 100M+ handmade listings signals ChatGPT's platform ambition for creative commerce.

products.name: Airbyte Agents — Context Store + MCP
products.gate_notes: user_can_perceive_today passes — available via MCP and SDK as of May 5. FAILS evidence_beyond_single_source: source_secondary is empty; sources are BusinessWire (a paid company press release wire, not independently written) and airbyte.com/blog (company-owned). Neither qualifies as an independent third-party source. No external media or community corroboration. May 5 is within window. Relevant as agent infrastructure signal — promote if independent coverage appears.
products.editorial_flag: watchlist — company-only sources. Monitor for TechCrunch/HN/VentureBeat coverage to promote.

original_record.name: Airbyte Agents — Context Store + MCP
original_record.launch_signal: May 5, 2026 launch — Airbyte Agents with Context Store addresses #1 root cause of production agent failure (data layer)
original_record.core_positioning: Pre-indexed unified data layer for AI agents that eliminates multi-API call chains and reduces token consumption
original_record.problem_it_solves: Most AI agent production failures stem from data failures (fragmented APIs, missing context) rather than model failures
original_record.user_visibility: Medium — developer/infrastructure tool. MCP compatibility means Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor users can access without direct Airbyte integration.
original_record.access_barrier_change: Available via MCP (low integration barrier for Claude/ChatGPT/Cursor users) + native SDK for custom agent teams
original_record.workflow_change: Pre-index Salesforce, Zendesk, Jira, Slack data into Context Store → agents query one endpoint instead of chaining 5-6 API calls → dramatically lower latency and token cost
original_record.distribution_change: MCP compatibility puts Airbyte's data layer inside Claude, ChatGPT, and Cursor without new integrations
original_record.new_product_form: AI agent data infrastructure layer (Context Store) positioned as the 'plumbing that makes agents reliable'
original_record.interaction_pattern: Agents query Context Store via MCP or SDK; Context Store handles freshness, search optimization, and multi-source consolidation transparently
user_scenarios: Agent team pulls customer context from Salesforce, Zendesk, Jira — 1 Context Store call instead of 3 separate API chains
Creative tool agent queries project history, asset library, team notes — unified via Context Store
original_record.business_model: Enterprise SaaS + open-source Airbyte core (data connectors). Context Store is a new premium feature.
original_record.risk_caveat: Infrastructure layer — success depends on connector coverage and data freshness guarantees. Enterprise data compliance (SOC2, GDPR) needs validation.

good: Addresses a real problem (data layer as root cause of agent failures) with strong resonance in developer community
bad: New product — no production track record yet

original_record.market_signal_strength: Medium — infrastructure signal; strong for teams building production agents but limited consumer visibility
original_record.relevance_to_us: High — directly relevant for our agent/workflow infrastructure. Context Store pattern (pre-indexed, unified data layer) is a key design consideration for our skill system's data access layer.
original_record.one_line_judgment: Airbyte launches a unified pre-indexed Context Store on May 5 — collapses 5-6 agent API calls into one and integrates via MCP with Claude, ChatGPT, and Cursor.

products.name: Airbyte — Rowboat AI Work App (Startup)
products.gate_notes: user_can_perceive_today passes (early access public launch). FAILS evidence_beyond_single_source: only hunted.space (community aggregator, not independent media), no corroborating official product page or community coverage. Also outside collection window (Apr 21, 8 days before window start). 86 upvotes on Product Hunt is modest. Retain as startup signal for persistent AI context management pattern.
products.editorial_flag: watchlist — single community source, outside window. Startup signal only.

original_record.name: Airbyte — Rowboat AI Work App (Startup)
original_record.launch_signal: Product Hunt launch April 21, 2026 — 86 upvotes, #26 for the day. Living knowledge graph for meetings, emails, and notes.
original_record.core_positioning: AI work app that builds a living knowledge graph from meetings, emails, and notes to handle tasks without re-explanation
original_record.problem_it_solves: Users must re-explain context to AI every session; meeting notes, emails, project context scattered across tools
original_record.user_visibility: Low — Product Hunt launch, indie product
original_record.access_barrier_change: Public launch on Product Hunt. Early access product.
original_record.workflow_change: Automatic context capture from meetings/emails/notes → AI that handles drafting, note-taking, project management without re-explaining
original_record.new_product_form: Persistent AI work assistant with living knowledge graph
original_record.interaction_pattern: Connect meeting notes and email → AI builds context graph → query or delegate tasks in natural language
user_scenarios: Creative director gets meeting notes automatically added to project context — delegates follow-up emails to Rowboat without re-briefing
original_record.business_model: Unknown (early access)
original_record.risk_caveat: Very early stage — indie Product Hunt launch. 86 upvotes is modest signal. Outside collection window (Apr 21).

good: 86 upvotes on Product Hunt shows genuine interest in the problem
bad: Very early stage
Outside collection window

original_record.relevance_to_us: Medium — knowledge graph pattern for persistent AI context is relevant to our skill/workflow memory system design.
original_record.one_line_judgment: Rowboat is an indie AI work app building a living knowledge graph from meetings and emails — a startup signal for persistent AI context management.

products.name: Submit.DIY — AI Launch Platform for Indie Makers (Startup)
products.gate_notes: user_can_perceive_today passes (public launch). FAILS evidence_beyond_single_source: only hunted.space, no independent media or official product page corroboration. Also outside collection window (Apr 17, 12 days before window start). 214 upvotes / #5 Product Hunt is a stronger signal than Rowboat. Retain as startup diversity signal for AI-enabled indie maker tooling category.
products.editorial_flag: watchlist — single community source, outside window. Startup diversity signal only.

original_record.name: Submit.DIY — AI Launch Platform for Indie Makers (Startup)
original_record.launch_signal: Product Hunt launch April 17, 2026 — 214 upvotes, #5 for the day. All-in-one AI launch platform for makers.
original_record.core_positioning: AI-generated launch copy + tracking across 160+ platforms for indie makers and small teams
original_record.problem_it_solves: Indie makers spend weeks manually writing launch copy for each platform (Product Hunt, HN, Twitter, etc.) and lose track of submissions
original_record.user_visibility: Medium for indie maker community — #5 on Product Hunt is strong signal
original_record.access_barrier_change: Public product. Indie/solo founder price point.
original_record.workflow_change: Input product details → AI generates ready-to-publish copy for 160+ channels → track all submissions in dashboard
original_record.distribution_change: Targets indie maker distribution strategy; not a big-tech product
original_record.new_product_form: AI-powered launch toolkit for indie makers
original_record.interaction_pattern: Product description → AI copy generation per platform → submission tracking dashboard
user_scenarios: Solo developer launches AI creative tool — uses Submit.DIY to generate 20 platform-specific descriptions and track all submissions
original_record.business_model: Unknown (early access / subscription)
original_record.risk_caveat: Outside collection window (Apr 17). Indie/solo — limited team and resources. Niche product for makers, not mainstream creative tool.

good: 214 upvotes / #5 Product Hunt is strong community validation for a maker tool
bad: Niche use case
Outside collection window
